data.title: Walmart Stock (WMT) Plunges After Q4 Earnings, but Jefferies Says Buy the Dip
data.description:
Walmart (WMT) just posted solid fourth-quarter results, yet its stock took a 6% hit. Investors reacted to the company’s cautious 2025 outlook, but Jefferies sees this as an overreaction. The firm is urging investors to buy the dip, arguing that Walmart’s guidance is conservative but easy to beat, just like in 2024, when the company kept topping forecasts.

Discover the Best Stocks and Maximize Your Portfolio:

  • See what stocks are receiving strong buy ratings from top-rated analysts.
  • Filter, analyze, and streamline your search for investment opportunities with TipRanks’ Stock Screener.

Strong Performance but Conservative Outlook

Walmart reported $180.55 billion in Q4 revenues, up 4.1% year-over-year. U.S. same-store sales climbed 4.6%, and global e-commerce sales surged 16%. Also, adjusted EPS came in at $0.66, up 10% from the same period last year and broadly in line with estimates. 

Even with these strong numbers, Walmart’s stock slid because the company’s guidance for the current year missed expectations. Walmart forecast annual net sales to rise in the range of 3% to 4% in the current Fiscal year, compared with analysts’ expectations of 4% growth. 

Jefferies wasn’t surprised by the cautious outlook but believes it sets the stage for a strong year of “beats & raises.”

Why Jefferies Thinks the Stock Will Rebound

Jefferies reiterated its Buy rating on the stock, highlighting Walmart’s continued market share gains, particularly among higher-income shoppers. The firm expects Walmart’s stock to bounce back soon, noting that its 16% year-to-date gain shows investors still believe in its long-term strength despite the recent drop.

Jefferies highlighted that the company’s margins remained stable due to smart inventory management, fewer markdowns, and solid pricing power. While operating income missed estimates, the firm pointed out that Walmart still managed to beat expectations on adjusted EPS.

Overall, Jefferies believes Walmart is well-positioned for strong earnings growth in 2025, making this pullback a buying opportunity. 

Is WMT a Good Stock to Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on WMT stock based on 24 Buys and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 70% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WMT price target of $112.25 per share implies 15.47% upside potential.

See more WMT analyst ratings 

Disclosure
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Preps to Host OpenAI’s GPT-4.5 and GPT-5 Models
data.description:
Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) is prepping to host OpenAI’s GPT-4.5 and GPT-5 AI (artificial intelligence) models on its Azure platform. The news was first reported by The Verge. Microsoft engineers are reportedly boosting server capacity for the launch of these generative AI models. OpenAI founder Sam Altman recently mentioned that GPT-4.5 could be launched as soon as the end of February. Meanwhile, The Verge report said that GPT-5 could be launched in late May. Microsoft has poured billions into OpenAI and has been one of the early and large backers of the ChatGPT maker.

Discover the Best Stocks and Maximize Your Portfolio:

  • See what stocks are receiving strong buy ratings from top-rated analysts.
  • Filter, analyze, and streamline your search for investment opportunities with TipRanks’ Stock Screener.

GPT-4.5 and GPT-5 Loaded with Advanced Features

GPT-4.5, also called Orion, is expected to be loaded with OpenAI’s most advanced offerings. Orion will be the startup’s last non-chain-of-thought model. Unlike a chain-of-thought (CoT) model, a non-CoT model does not break down the query into logical steps before giving a final response. Instead, it directly jumps to the final answer in order to generate a faster response. A CoT model is preferred as it breaks down the query into a series of steps, allowing it to improve the model’s reasoning capabilities.

Meanwhile, GPT-5 is expected to combine a lot of OpenAI’s technology into a single system. Altman is excited about GPT-5 as it is also going to integrate OpenAI’s latest o3 reasoning model. OpenAI launched the o3-mini model in January but has decided not to release the large o3 model as a standalone offering. Instead, it will be integrated into the GPT-5 system. GPT-5 will also unify OpenAI’s o-series and GPT-series models so that users do not have to pick between the two to know which model is better for them.

OpenAI Moves a Step Closer to AGI

OpenAI is on a mission to create an artificial general intelligence (AGI) model, and GPT-5 is taking the company one step closer to this goal. AGI is referred to as the hypothetical intelligence of a large language model (LLM) that has the ability to think, self-teach (learn), act, and detect problems more closely like a human.

As far as pricing for the GPT-5 goes, Altman said in a February 12 post that the free tier will give users unlimited access to chat at the “standard intelligence setting.” At the same time, Plus users will have access to a “higher level of intelligence” of GPT-5, and Pro subscribers will be able to access “at an even higher level of intelligence.”

Is Microsoft a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street is highly optimistic about Microsoft stock’s trajectory. On TipRanks, MSFT stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 29 Buys and three Hold ratings. Also, the average Microsoft stock price target of $510.38 implies 22.7% upside potential from current levels. In the past year, MSFT stock has gained 4.1%.

See more MSFT analyst ratings

Disclosure
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Alibaba Soars 8% as Ryan Cohen Bets $1B Following Q3 Earnings Beat
data.description:
Chinese e-commerce retailer Alibaba (BABA) stock spiked over 8% after billionaire investor Ryan Cohen increased his stake to approximately $1 billion, according to The Wall Street Journal. His move comes soon after Alibaba reported strong Q3 FY25 financial results, which beat expectations on both revenue and profit. A strong domestic e-commerce market, cloud expansion, and AI momentum fueled the company’s comeback, signaling renewed investor confidence.

Discover the Best Stocks and Maximize Your Portfolio:

  • See what stocks are receiving strong buy ratings from top-rated analysts.
  • Filter, analyze, and streamline your search for investment opportunities with TipRanks’ Stock Screener.

Billionaires Are Doubling Down on Alibaba

Alibaba stock has already jumped over 60% this year, and Cohen’s buy-in could push even more investors to jump in. Cohen, famed for sparking stock surges at GameStop (GME) and Chewy (CHWY), has a sharp eye for undervalued opportunities. Now, with roughly 7 million shares in hand, he is making it clear that he sees Alibaba as a high-upside play in China’s recovery. His investment could pay off as the economy stabilizes and investor confidence returns to the tech sector.

He’s not the only one making big moves. Billionaire hedge fund manager David Tepper also ramped up his Alibaba position, with his firm, Appaloosa Management, boosting its stake to over $1 billion by the end of 2024. This investment cements Alibaba as one of Tepper’s largest holdings, further fueling optimism around the stock.

Alibaba’s Revival Is Picking up Speed

Alibaba has been on a roll, posting its fastest revenue growth in over a year. Also, the company is doubling down on AI, with the recent launch of Qwen 2.5 Max, an advanced model it claims outperforms DeepSeek-V3.

For Q3, Alibaba reported an 8% increase in revenue to 280.2 billion yuan ($38.38 billion), marking its most rapid growth since late 2023. At the same time, Alibaba’s cloud division continues to draw attention. In Q3, cloud revenues rose 13% year-on-year to 31.7 billion yuan, $4.3 billion, which BABA said was primarily driven by the growing adoption of AI-related products.

The confidence in Chinese tech stocks is reviving back and Alibaba seems to lead the charge.

Is BABA Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

With Cohen’s billion-dollar bet and Alibaba’s accelerating momentum, the stock is once again a must-watch for investors. Turning to Wall Street, the analysts’ consensus for Alibaba is Strong Buy based on nine Buys and one Hold rating over the last three months. At $130.80, the average BABA price target implies a 3.80% downside potential.

See more BABA analyst ratings

Disclosure
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Google’s YouTube Premium Lite Is Coming Soon
data.description:
Alphabet-owned (GOOGL) Google’s YouTube Premium Lite is coming soon to the U.S., Australia, Germany, and Thailand. The news was first reported by Bloomberg. The new subscription plan is expected to cost nearly half of the current YouTube Premium price of $12.99 per month but will not have some perks such as access to ad-free YouTube Music (Premium), offline downloads, and background playback.

Discover the Best Stocks and Maximize Your Portfolio:

  • See what stocks are receiving strong buy ratings from top-rated analysts.
  • Filter, analyze, and streamline your search for investment opportunities with TipRanks’ Stock Screener.

YouTube Premium Lite Could Cost Half the Price

The new cheaper subscription plan is aimed at users who usually wish to see content other than music videos. Nonetheless, users would have ad-free access to “most videos,” the report stated. The plan will offer subscribers an ad-free experience and access to a massive library of how-to content and podcasts. In the U.S., the current YouTube Premium plan is priced at $13.99 per month, and the YouTube Music Premium plan costs $10.99 a month. The exact pricing of the new Premium Lite version remains unknown at the moment.

YouTube had tested a similar model earlier between 2021 and 2023 but had no success with it in Europe. This time around, YouTube has been testing the subscription model in some markets, including Australia, since a couple of months. The Premium Lite offering costs AU$8.99 per month, while the regular YouTube Premium costs AU$16.99 per month in Australia.  

In Fiscal 2024, YouTube generated $36.15 billion in advertising revenues, up 14.7% compared to FY23. The new subscription service, if successful, could add more subscription revenues, making it a powerful contributor other than ad revenues.

Is Google Stock a Good Buy?

Wall Street remains divided on Alphabet stock due to the company’s massive AI investments. On TipRanks, GOOGL stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 27 Buys and 10 Hold ratings. Also, the average Alphabet price target of $215.85 implies a nearly 17% upside potential from current levels.

See more GOOGL analyst ratings

Disclosure
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Tesla EVs Set to Hit Indian Roads as First Shipment Nears
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is all set to roll out its EVs in India, as the first shipment is reportedly expected to arrive soon. Bloomberg, citing sources, reported that the U.S.-based EV giant plans to ship thousands of cars to a port near Mumbai in the next few months, opening up its brand to the world’s biggest population. The news follows Elon Musk’s meeting with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi at the White House last week, after which Tesla officials announced plans to start hiring in India.

Discover the Best Stocks and Maximize Your Portfolio:

  • See what stocks are receiving strong buy ratings from top-rated analysts.
  • Filter, analyze, and streamline your search for investment opportunities with TipRanks’ Stock Screener.

Tesla Moves Closer to India Launch

With these latest moves, Tesla seems ready to launch the brand in the Indian market. After the shipment, Tesla is expected to focus on establishing its manufacturing unit in India. According to a Times of India (ToI) report, the company contacted several state governments, including Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, and Maharashtra, for potential factory locations. Moreover, Tesla has reportedly approached Indian automobile company Tata Motors’ (IN:TATAMOTORS) executives for a potential collaboration.

Tesla plans to build a manufacturing plant with a capacity of 500,000 vehicles, investing between $2 billion and $3 billion. In its initial proposal to the government, the company outlined plans to produce a brand-new budget EV for India, expected to be priced between ₹20-25 lakh. Reports further suggest that Tesla plans to sell its cars in Indian cities like New Delhi, Mumbai, and Bengaluru by the third quarter of the year.

Furthermore, Tesla plans to send its cars to India from its factory in Germany, not from China, even though China is much closer to India. The ToI report stated that Tesla chose this longer route because the Indian government had some concerns about shipping from China.

Tesla’s entry into India has been tough due to high import taxes. However, a recent customs duty cut in India from 110% to 70% on cars over $40,000 could ease Tesla’s journey ahead.

Is Tesla a Good Stock to Buy?

On Wall Street, analysts have maintained a neutral stance on Tesla stock. According to TipRanks, TSLA stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with 13 Buy, 12 Hold, and 10 Sell recommendations. The average price target for Tesla shares is $351.38, which is almost similar to the current level.

See more TSLA analyst ratings

Disclosure
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: ‘Buy In as Catalysts Approach,’ Says Daniel Ives About Tesla Stock
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) could be losing a significant portion of its customer base – those who now hesitate to buy a car tied to Elon Musk. However, that doesn’t seem to dent its long-term potential, at least according to Tesla bull and Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives.

Discover the Best Stocks and Maximize Your Portfolio:

  • See what stocks are receiving strong buy ratings from top-rated analysts.
  • Filter, analyze, and streamline your search for investment opportunities with TipRanks’ Stock Screener.

Ives concedes Musk’s involvement with DOGE and his “powerful alliance” with Trump could potentially drive some consumers away from the Tesla brand, and that there is “some momentum around this effort in Europe and pockets of the US.” However, he is not all that worried that it will have a big impact on the company.

“We view this as containable brand issues for Tesla for now that are not a major cause for concern,” the analyst went on to say.

Another less ideological issue that Ives thinks is worrying Tesla investors is that Musk is devoting so much time to DOGE that it is distracting him from Tesla at a critical moment for the company. With intense competition in autonomous and robotics markets in both the U.S. and China, a distracted Musk is a bad look. Yet, Ives remains sanguine here too, saying, “The bears will now focus on this DOGE issue, isolated protests, brand worries as their latest narrative, but the reality is this does not change the future of Tesla.”

And the future of Tesla, according to Ives, involves multiple projects that are about to come to fruition. Tesla is preparing for a new mass-market vehicle launch in the first half of 2025, making “major product developments” in autonomy and Optimus, planning the Austin unsupervised FSD launch for June, while there are also a “host of other growth EV/battery catalysts on the horizon.”

With a cheaper model set to be released, Ives thinks “modest delivery growth” is in the cards for this year, while the near-term offers other events to look forward to as well.

“We believe a new vehicle launch in the coming week with Model Y Juniper along with the Austin FSD unsupervised kick off in June will be key events and catalysts as investors focus on Tesla’s next chapter of growth and the Street adjusts to a Musk that will successfully (in our view) balance his Tesla CEO role along with DOGE, SpaceX, xAI, and a myriad of other initiatives,” Ives summed up.

Accordingly, Ives rates Tesla shares as an Outperform (i.e., Buy), along with a Street-high $550 price target, implying a 55% upside from current levels. (To watch Ives’ track record, click here)

However, most on the Street take a more skeptical stance; while 12 analysts join Musk in the bull camp, with an additional 12 Holds and 10 Sells, the consensus view is that this stock is a Hold (i.e., Neutral). The $351.38 average price target suggests shares will stay range-bound for the foreseeable future. (See TSLA stock forecast)

To find good ideas for stocks trading at attractive valuations, visit TipRanks’ Best Stocks to Buy, a tool that unites all of TipRanks’ equity insights.

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured analyst. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Daniel Ives Dismisses Palantir Stock Drop as a ‘Knee-Jerk Overreaction’
data.description:
Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) shares were under pressure for the second consecutive day on Thursday following an announcement that investors interpreted as bad news for the high-flying big data company.

Discover the Best Stocks and Maximize Your Portfolio:

  • See what stocks are receiving strong buy ratings from top-rated analysts.
  • Filter, analyze, and streamline your search for investment opportunities with TipRanks’ Stock Screener.

Specifically, on Wednesday, the Trump Administration announced a new five-year plan to reduce defense spending by 8% per year (approximately $290 billion). With Palantir’s business heavily reliant on government contracts, investors are obviously fearful Palantir will be receiving less government work over the next few years.

However, Wedbush analyst and long-time Palantir bull Daniel Ives thinks it all amounts to a knee-jerk overreaction with the detractors clutching at straws.

“The bears that have hated Palantir from $12 to $120 in the last 18 months now have found their latest ‘silver bullet’ negative thesis around PLTR being exposed to these budget cuts and we saw a sharp sell-off in trading accordingly once this news about DOD hit,” the analyst said.

Thing is, says Ives, the news should be looked at in an entirely different light. The analyst believes the DoD cuts will have the opposite effect, as Palantir’s “unique software approach” is likely to secure more IT budget allocations at the Pentagon – not less.

“Palantir is so well positioned for this new disciplined spending environment at the Pentagon and this will ultimately be a positive growth catalyst as the various programs are scrutinized and as Karp & Co. get a bigger seat at the table in the Beltway,” Ives further said.

The increased AI investments under the Trump Administration, particularly through Project Stargate, should be beneficial for Palantir as more organizations seek to “implement strategic AI infrastructure.” Additionally, the company’s FedRAMP (Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program) high authorization for all its cloud services portfolio allows the company to offer its products across the entire U.S. government, including highly sensitive workloads.

As such, with Palantir gaining momentum across both federal and commercial sectors, Ives continues to view it as a top stock to own in 2025, seeing this sell-off as “another opportunity.” The analyst believes Palantir has the potential to reach a trillion-dollar market cap in the years ahead, positioning itself as the next Oracle or Salesforce amid the unfolding AI revolution.

“We view this latest worry as noise given where we see PLTR’s bright AI driven future,” Ives summed up.

Bottom line, Ives remains on PLTR’s side, assigning an Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating for the shares, along with a $120 price target. Should the figure be met, investors will be pocketing returns of 20% a year from now. (To watch Ives’ track record, click here)

As a whole, the Street is less enamored with this name. Based on a mix of 10 Holds, 5 Sells and 3 Buys, the analyst consensus rates the stock a Hold (i.e., Neutral). Meanwhile, going by the $91.88 average price target, shares are currently overvalued by ~14%. (See PLTR stock forecast)

To find good ideas for stocks trading at attractive valuations, visit TipRanks’ Best Stocks to Buy, a tool that unites all of TipRanks’ equity insights.

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured analyst. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.

data.title: BKNG Earnings: Booking Holdings’ Financial Results Top Estimates Across the Board
data.description:
Online travel agency Booking Holdings (BKNG) has reported fourth-quarter 2024 financial results that beat Wall Street’s expectations across the board.

Discover the Best Stocks and Maximize Your Portfolio:

  • See what stocks are receiving strong buy ratings from top-rated analysts.
  • Filter, analyze, and streamline your search for investment opportunities with TipRanks’ Stock Screener.

The Connecticut-based company announced earnings per share (EPS) of $41.55. That was far ahead of the $36.08 that was the consensus expectation of analysts who track the company’s progress. Revenue in the final months of 2024 totaled $5.47 billion, beating forecasts that called for $5.19 billion.

For all of last year, the company reported EPS of $172.69 on revenue of $23.74 billion. Management attributed the strong to booming demand for international travel. In particular, leisure travel in Europe, the company’s largest market, has remained strong, led by domestic and long-haul trips from American travelers.

Rising Demand in Asia

Travel demand has also been rising in Asia-Pacific, with destinations in Southeast Asia benefiting from an influx of Chinese tourists after the lifting of some visa restrictions. Total room nights booked through Booking Holdings increased 13% from a year earlier in Q4 2024 to 261 million. The company posted gross bookings of $37.2 billion, up about 17% from a year earlier.

Booking Holdings runs several travel websites, including Booking.com, Priceline.com, Kayak, Cheapflights, and Rentalcars.com, to name only a few. The company operates websites in 40 languages across 200 countries. Over the last 12 months, BKNG stock has risen 38% over the past 12 months.

Is BKNG Stock a Buy?

The stock of Booking Holdings has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 21 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 15 Buy and six Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average BKNG price target of $5,446.37 implies 8.53% upside from current levels. These ratings are likely to change after the latest financial results.

Read more analyst ratings on BKNG stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: RIVN Earnings: Rivian Delivers Strong Results but Offers Weak Guidance
data.description:
Electric vehicle maker Rivian Automotive (RIVN) has delivered better-than-expected financial results but offered weak forward guidance.

Discover the Best Stocks and Maximize Your Portfolio:

  • See what stocks are receiving strong buy ratings from top-rated analysts.
  • Filter, analyze, and streamline your search for investment opportunities with TipRanks’ Stock Screener.

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Rivian announced a gross profit of $170 million, which was more than double the $64 million expected on Wall Street. The company reported an earnings per share (EPS) loss of -$0.46, which was better than estimates of a -$0.68 loss. Revenue in the period totaled $1.7 billion, which topped the $1.5 billion consensus estimate of analysts.

Rivian said that its profit was about $12,000 per vehicle, and that it delivered 14,183 cars in the final quarter of 2024, up from 13,972 a year earlier. However, while the gross profit and sales figures were strong, the company’s 2025 guidance was soft.

Weak Outlook

Looking out at this year, Rivian said it expects to deliver 46,000 to 51,000 vehicles. The midpoint of that range, 48,500, is below Wall Street projections of about 55,000 vehicles. Rivian delivered almost 52,000 electric vehicles in 2024, which means the company is expecting a decline in its deliveries this year.

In its earnings release, Rivian said that it is still planning to launch its cheaper R2 electric vehicle by the first half of 2026. Analysts have said that a lower cost EV is critically important to Rivian’s future as the company’s current R1T electric pick-up truck has an expensive starting price of $70,000.

RIVN stock has declined 12% over the last year.

Is RIVN Stock a Buy?

The stock of Rivian Automotive has a consensus Hold rating among 11 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on two Buy, seven Hold, and two Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average RIVN price target of $13.74 implies 0.96% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on RIVN stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: NerdWallet (NRDS) Shares Plunge on Concerns Over Drop in Users
data.description:
Despite an impressive Q4 2024 performance report that included top and bottom-line beats, NerdWallet (NRDS) has experienced a nearly 20% one-day drop in share price as market participants honed in on the projected ongoing decline in monthly users. 

Discover the Best Stocks and Maximize Your Portfolio:

  • See what stocks are receiving strong buy ratings from top-rated analysts.
  • Filter, analyze, and streamline your search for investment opportunities with TipRanks’ Stock Screener.

A Troubling Trend

NerdWallet, an online provider of personal finance advice and financial products, reportedly exceeded fourth-quarter expectations by growing its revenue 37% year-over-year, reaching $184 million and achieving $17 million in non-GAAP operating income. The firm credits its overall success partly to the robust demand in its Insurance sector, which witnessed an 800% year-over-year surge, and to a 5% increase in its banking products.

However, due to rising 10-year rates, NerdWallet faced consumer and SMB lending challenges. Although the company expanded its mortgage business by 4%, the almost 80 basis rise in 30-year mortgage rates has moderated its growth expectations for this sector. The personal loans division saw a 51% decrease year-over-year as efforts were redirected toward Insurance. Yet, early indications suggest a return to growth in Q1 of 2025 in response to shifting resources to meet increasing demand.

The company reported a 20% drop in Monthly Unique Users (MUUs) in Q4 and anticipates continued challenges in organic traffic growth toward non-monetizing pages, though the firm expects stabilization and a return to growth by early 2026.

NerdWallet has announced it is shifting away from focusing on the number of relationships rather than their quality, as shown by its decision to move away from its Monthly Unique User (MUU) disclosure. The company believes converting even a small percentage of existing mortgage traffic into brokering relations is more valuable than triple education-oriented mortgage traffic.

Analysts Remain Bullish, But Questions Linger

Analysts following the company have mostly remained bullish on the stock. Yet, KeyBanc’s five-star analyst Justin Petterson lowered the price target on NerdWallet’s shares to $18 (from $20) while keeping an Overweight rating, noting the company outperformed expected revenue growth for Q4 and expects growth tailwinds to continue into Q1 2025. However, a softer margin outlook and diminishing traffic growth invite questions about potentially shifting unit economics.

It is a trend investors will want to monitor moving forward.

Disclosure
data.author: Casey Dylan, CIMA
